key_insight: A ceramic thermal barrier coating combined with a Jatropha biodiesel-water-butanol emulsion, especially B20W10Bu10, improved diesel engine efficiency while reducing major exhaust emissions.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that low-heat-rejection engine coatings can work synergistically with oxygenated alternative fuels to boost performance and cut pollution in compression-ignition engines.
method: Applied a 100 µm NiCrAlY bond coat and 200 µm 8YSZ top coat by air plasma spraying.
Prepared ternary emulsions (B20W10Bu5, B20W10Bu10, B20W10Bu15) using ultrasonic homogenization.
Tested coated vs. conventional diesel engine performance, combustion, and emissions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that B20W10Bu10 increased brake thermal efficiency by 7.4% and reduced brake-specific fuel consumption by 7.8% relative to the conventional coated diesel engine, with lower HC, CO, and smoke emissions.
watch_item: Emulsion stability, long-term coating durability, and real-engine operability need validation before practical deployment.
paper_title: Effect of Ceramic Thermal Barrier Coatings on a Diesel Engine Fueled with Jatropha Biodiesel Ternary Emulsion Blends
